$comment: SMGP team-season history capsules, season 11 (SMGP-024). Base-universe history: the world's own arc, NOT the player career. DISPLAY-ONLY, never a fold input.

team.madonna.summary: A rulebook built to blunt him was, in the end, just another rulebook. Senna read the one-allocation law once, understood it was a craft examination, and reminded the paddock that craft was always his country too. The MADONNA 456's engineers turned the PALM 190 V10's torque curve into a tyre-nursing instrument, and the yellow-and-red garage spent the season making rationing look like privilege. Asselin banked the door-side arithmetic with his usual immaculate silence. The mood never flickered. The crown does not garden, said the pit wall; it simply lets the others wear their rubber out reaching.
team.madonna.objective: Bend the allocation law to the same serene arithmetic as every law before it.
team.madonna.definingEvent: Senna converts the first one-allocation weekend into a demonstration the whole pit lane photographs.
team.madonna.carryForward: The benchmark stands above the coming family quarrel exactly as it stands above everything else.

team.firenze.summary: Elssler called the allocation era the first honest rulebook, then went out and raced it like a man proving a point. The FIRENZE 500 flowered under the ration: the FIRENZE 99 V12's flexibility nursed rubber better than any rival's muscle, and the #3 spent the season stalking the crown with the tyre-whispering precision the horsepower years had wasted. Germi supplied the opera, one rapturous Sunday at a time, though even the tifosi noticed the ice was the faster religion now. The garage's only open file was an early-season brake-temperature puzzle it closed by summer.
team.firenze.objective: Stalk the crown through the craft formula the horsepower years denied him.
team.firenze.definingEvent: Elssler declares the allocation era the first honest rulebook, and drives it like a homeland.
team.firenze.carryForward: The ice has a formula worth defending now, and the autumn of the veterans will need it.

team.millions.summary: For one season, the chequebook was the slowest part of the car. No invoice could buy a fresh set of rubber, so the MILLIONS 189 had to be driven like a ledger, and the garage discovered two very different accountants in its cockpits. Alberti nursed with patrician disdain for waste, closing stints like estates; Jones spent his ration like a man spending someone else's fortune, fast and brief. The DICK MD V10's thirst for tyre life was the winter's real enemy, only half-tamed by a new differential map. The gold learned to garden, and hated every weed.
team.millions.objective: Prove the chrome garage can win an argument no amount of gold can settle.
team.millions.definingEvent: Alberti's closing-stint masterclass at mid-season, driven on rubber every rival had already killed.
team.millions.carryForward: The patrician's testimonial year approaches, and the garage owes him a car worthy of the bunting.

team.bestowal.summary: The ration suited the marble house's manners better than its machinery. Blume treated a fixed tyre allocation the way he treats everything, as a correctness exercise, and produced the season's tidiest stint sheets outside Spain. Picos, warmer blood, had to learn to slow his hands before the rulebook slowed them for him. The BESTOWAL 167's trouble was the VAPOR DN's delivery, bred for cathedrals rather than allotments, and the winter package that softened it arrived half a season late. The cathedral kept its dignity, banked its usual haul, and filed the gardening era under things to outlast.
team.bestowal.objective: Out-correct the ration until the power formula comes home.
team.bestowal.definingEvent: Blume's stint sheets become the second most photographed documents in the paddock, after Pacheco's.
team.bestowal.carryForward: The benefactor's engineers are promised a new formula on the drawing board, and start saving for it.

team.blanche.summary: This was the hour the whitecoats were founded for. One allocation of rubber, sixteen Grands Prix of arithmetic, and Herbin drove the season like a man reading his own handwriting aloud: stint after spotless stint, the BLANCHE 582 parked in the establishment's mirrors until the LEVEL A door audibly creaked. The DELTA 103 V10's thrift made the white car the benchmark of the ration, and the aristocrats began pulling his laps apart frame by frame. White grafted cheerfully in support. The year's only stain was sharing the craft argument with Hamano and Pacheco, and the garage stopped minding by autumn.
team.blanche.objective: Make the ration year the white car's finest, and turn best-of-the-rest into an establishment problem.
team.blanche.definingEvent: Herbin runs a full stint faster on old rubber than the leaders manage on new.
team.blanche.carryForward: The white car has the establishment's attention at last, and intends to keep it while the sisters quarrel.

team.tyrant.summary: The crossover blade became the season's meta, and the gunmetal garage nearly smiled. Hamano's forty laps of patience were suddenly the sharpest weapon on the grid, the #11 banking and banking until the TYRANT 548 stood as the steadiest operation in the house's life. Gould, denied his walls by the gardening, channelled the island nerve into late, surgical charges the ration never saw coming. The LIZZIE 24 V8's even temper made the car kind to its rubber. The boot camp's discovery was unexpected: discipline had been the creed all along. The menace simply learned to wait.
team.tyrant.objective: Bank the steadiest season in the house's history on the patience it always secretly had.
team.tyrant.definingEvent: Hamano's forty-lap closing stint becomes the season's textbook page.
team.tyrant.carryForward: Two weapons, one philosophy: the boot camp enters the family-war year as the grid's calmest garage.

team.losel.summary: The professor's year. At Spain, Pacheco nursed his allocation with a watchmaker's calm and turned his home round into the season's lecture hall; every pit wall on the grid photographed his stint sheets. The LOSEL 125 had been built for this since the first slide rule, and the VAPOR DNPQ V8's newly filed fuel curve completed the equation. Dehehe's blue metronome closed smaller sums in support, points banked and never handed back. The data door's season was not flawless, two setup proofs failed in the spring, but it ended with the paddock admitting the arithmetic had become the sport.
team.losel.objective: Teach the whole grid the season's only subject, and grade them at the flag.
team.losel.definingEvent: Spain becomes the lecture hall: Pacheco's stint sheets are photographed by every pit wall.
team.losel.carryForward: The data men turn their slide rules toward the sister-house war, the grid's best case study.

team.may.summary: Optimism met the one subject it had never studied: patience. Turner's instinct was to spend the car early and smile later, and the one-allocation law punished exactly that, so the leaf-green garage spent the spring learning to garden and the autumn harvesting what it had sown. The MAY 555's LORRY 32 V8 stayed loyally within its means, and the compressed field still let the #15 mug the occasional aristocrat on Sundays when the gardening was done early. The crew whistled through the frustration. The season's lesson, chalked on the garage wall, read: the sun helps those who water first.
team.may.objective: Learn patience without losing the grin.
team.may.definingEvent: Turner cooks his ration in the Preliminary Race at the opener, then never does it again.
team.may.carryForward: The youngest crew on the grid has a new subject passed, and spring comes earlier now.

team.bullets.summary: Hands were always his currency, and the ration year was a drivers' market. Ceara treated the one-allocation law as a personal invitation, nursing the featherweight BULLETS 560 through stints that made the aristocrats' timing screens flicker with disbelief. The backstreet crew, which had never been able to afford fresh-rubber arrogance, found the whole grid suddenly living on its budget. The hand-fettled LIZZIE 24 V8 gave its usual honest thunder. No protest was lodged this year; the old war with Dardan slept. The insurgent banked his best stretch of the Charter era and began, very quietly, to plan.
team.bullets.objective: Turn the drivers' market into the insurgent's best Charter campaign yet.
team.bullets.definingEvent: Ceara nurses one allocation past two works teams in a single closing stint.
team.bullets.carryForward: The plan forming in the backstreet lock-up will soon require everything the garage owns.

team.dardan.summary: The spear learned to garden, and surprised itself by being good at it. Alfven's frozen patience was built for a tyre ration, and the #19 strung together the cleanest run of finishes in the oxblood house's memory. Bellini fought the discipline for half a season, then submitted to it with the fury of a man who hates being taught, and his late-year stints were the best of his Charter years. The DARDAN 700's VAPOR DNPQ V8 minded its thirst. The grudge across the pit lane stayed silent, and the clan discovered that silence, properly maintained, is also a weapon.
team.dardan.objective: Prove the oxblood garage can win the arithmetic as well as the argument.
team.dardan.definingEvent: Alfven's finishing run becomes the house record, built entirely on patience.
team.dardan.carryForward: Bellini has one homecoming left in him, and Italy has been waiting longer than he knows.

team.linden.summary: The paddock's philosopher had been gardening for twenty years before the rulebook made it fashionable. Moreau nursed the LINDEN LN198 through the ration with the serenity of a man who had seen every formula pass, and the old green car's finishing column was the envy of garages with ten times its budget. The LORRY 32 V8 sipped where others gulped. The garage's one technical file was an early tyre-temperature puzzle the veterans solved with chalk and patience. The root kept growing, the watch kept being set, and the pit lane's elders came, one by one, to ask how.
team.linden.objective: Bank another harvest, and lend the ration era the benefit of two decades of patience.
team.linden.definingEvent: Half the pit lane queues at the green garage for Moreau's notes on tyre age.
team.linden.carryForward: The elder's authority is now technical as well as moral, and the veterans' autumn will use both.

team.minarae.summary: The reckoning arrived, and the forge came out stronger. One scenery-strike too many, and the garage told Nono to his face what everyone had been saying for years; he went silent, no interviews, no bravado, just laps. Miller told the press the boy had finally started listening to the tyres instead of the crowd. The return was the year's quiet revelation: patient into hairpins, kind to rubber, the #21 banking his first sustained run of points. The MINARAE 594 and SEGA SG1000 V8 had been capable all along. The punchline, ten years late, started calling himself a racer.
team.minarae.objective: Survive the reckoning, and rebuild the heir before the talent curdles.
team.minarae.definingEvent: Nono's silent months end with the first sustained points run of his career.
team.minarae.carryForward: The forge has a racer now, and the paddock has stopped laughing at exactly the wrong time.

team.rigel.summary: Restorers understand rationing better than anyone; the museum had been nursing one set of everything for a decade. The RIGEL 3000's stately manners suited the allocation year perfectly, and Cotman and Chardin turned the gardening season into the house's second consecutive decent campaign, a streak no one in the garage dared name aloud. The LORRY 32 V8's age became a virtue, its delivery gentle as restoration glue. Cotman's finishing count climbed toward numbers he had stopped dreaming about. The only shadow was the trophy shelf, dusted daily, reminding everyone what the light used to look like.
team.rigel.objective: Make two decent seasons in a row, and let the streak speak for the star.
team.rigel.definingEvent: The house records back-to-back respectable campaigns for the first time in living memory.
team.rigel.carryForward: The restorers now believe the new era has a place for them, and the belief is load-bearing.

team.comet.summary: Even the comet-chasers had a gardening year, and it nearly meant something. Tornio's silent economy with a tyre allocation was the garage's best-kept asset, the #29 nursing the COMET 323 through stints that outlasted cars with twice the budget, and the black car's finishing run stretched to a house record. The LIZZIE 24 V8 gave honest, unspectacular service. One grey afternoon the timing screen briefly showed the old name higher than it had any right to be, and the veterans of The Season allowed themselves, very briefly, to remember. Then it rained somewhere else, and normal service resumed.
team.comet.objective: Nurse the machine, stretch the streak, and keep the memory from embarrassing the present.
team.comet.definingEvent: One grey afternoon puts the black car briefly, wonderfully, higher than it should be.
team.comet.carryForward: The garage now measures hope in streaks, and the streak is alive.

team.orchis.summary: The ration was the flower's hardest examination yet, because nursing a tyre means enduring the shove, and the shove makes the bloom wilt. Tegner coaxed the ORCHIS 056 through the season with a gardener's patience, accepting small, cool harvests on the grey afternoons and conceding the hot ones with a pragmatism that nearly broke his heart. The MISFIRE 50 V8, at least, completed its second season without expiring. The glasshouse's ledgers recorded fewer points and more lessons than the year before, and one note underlined twice: the flower lasts longer when it stops racing the sun.
team.orchis.objective: Learn endurance, even if the tuition is paid in wilted Sundays.
team.orchis.definingEvent: Tegner concedes a hot afternoon deliberately and banks the cool one that follows.
team.orchis.carryForward: The glasshouse has a new doctrine, survival first, and the frost gambler has become its professor.

team.zeroforce.summary: The rumour from the winter had not yet come true, so the floor did what the floor does: counted. Klinger nursed the ZEROFORCE 231 through the ration with the patience of a man who counts laps like birthdays, and the old LORRY 32 V8, flattered by the gardening's gentler pace, reached the flag at a rate the chalk wall recorded as a house best. There were still Sundays parked on the grass, still cold coffee and gallows humour. But the arithmetic was improving, one finish at a time, and the crew began marking a date it dared not explain.
team.zeroforce.objective: Improve the arithmetic one finish at a time, and be alive when the rumour comes true.
team.zeroforce.definingEvent: The chalk wall records the best finishing rate in the house's history.
team.zeroforce.carryForward: The marked date is coming: the new formula, and with it, engines the floor can afford to finish.

team.joke.summary: With the establishment finally watching the #16 on Saturdays, Dufay had to invent a new act, and the ration year gave him one: conservation comedy. The carnival crew sandbagged its own sandbagging, parading fake tyre panic on the pit wall while the JOKE 777 nursed its allocation to the end and mugged the exhausted midfield in the last ten laps. The POND V8 ran thriftily enough to make the trick repeatable. The ledger held in LEVEL B, the grandstands howled, and the paddock's favourite punchline proved the joke could survive being understood, which is more than most comedians manage.
team.joke.objective: Invent a second act now that the whole paddock knows the first one.
team.joke.definingEvent: The fake tyre panic at mid-season fools an entire pit straight of strategists.
team.joke.carryForward: The carnival is armed for the family-war year, when everyone's attention will be elsewhere.

team.lares.summary: The gentleman's summer. Everything the hearth-red house believed, patience, courtesy, the car that comes home, became the season's whole curriculum, and Arai taught it with a small bow: the LARES 92 strung together the finest run of finishes in the garage's history, and the little mantel filled faster than ever before. The RAM V12's appetite, the one old quarrel, was half-tamed by winter fuel work. The paddock, which had snickered at the lamp-lit loyalty for years, came by the door to nod. The warmest story in racing belonged, for once, to the people who deserved it.
team.lares.objective: Turn the ration year into the warmest season the little house has ever kept.
team.lares.definingEvent: The finishing streak becomes the longest in Lares history, and the mantel fills.
team.lares.carryForward: The hearth-red car is now the paddock's proof that kindness can be a technical philosophy.

team.feet.summary: The invoice came due weekly, exactly as the grafters had predicted. The one-allocation law was honest work written into the rulebook, and the FEET 13 thrived: Rampal nursed, dragged and bullied the car through the ration with the economy of a man who had never been able to afford waste. The YOUGEN V10's thrift became a genuine asset, and Old Boots came home in the points with a regularity the horsepower years never permitted. The pit lane's caps stayed tipped. The garage chalked the season up as proof that the new era paid wages, and went straight back to work.
team.feet.objective: Collect every honest wage the ration year offers, down to the final bolt.
team.feet.definingEvent: Old Boots outlasts three richer cars in one rationed Sunday.
team.feet.carryForward: The grafters want a second tool in the box now: speed to go with the sweat.

team.serga.summary: The hand-built car kept writing its thank-you letters, and the ration year gave the mountain man a subject he understood: making things last. Sambena nursed the SERGA 1000 through the allocation with a welder's thrift, and the SC3000 F12, an engine built by men who rebuild what others bin, found the gardening era strangely hospitable. The thousand small names on the flanks got their money's worth, the #25 finishing with a regularity that embarrassed richer operations. The pit lane's favourite privateer ended the season with his best finishing column since the rescue, and not one cheque from a boardroom.
team.serga.objective: Make the rescued car last beautifully, and pay the paddock back in finishes.
team.serga.definingEvent: The #25 posts its best finishing column since the whip-round year.
team.serga.carryForward: The grey car's example is about to be borrowed at national scale by a garage with a bigger dream.

team.cool.summary: Fewer tyre changes meant more time in the deckchairs, and Cool approved in the strongest terms it ever uses, a raised glass. Delvaux nursed the COOL 05 through the ration with the dreamy economy of a man in no hurry whatsoever, and the CORSE V8 rewarded the light touch with the best finishing rate of the garage's recent memory. A top ten arrived on one golden evening and was celebrated until the light actually failed. The big board kept saying whatever it liked. The sunset, the garage noted, had never once needed a fresh set of anything.
team.cool.objective: Enjoy the calmest season the rulebook has ever offered, and bank what drifts by.
team.cool.definingEvent: One golden evening brings a top ten, celebrated until the light fails.
team.cool.carryForward: The deckchairs have outlasted another formula argument, and will outlast the next one too.

team.moon.summary: The orbit tightened another degree. The ration year was kind to machinery that could not afford aggression, and the MOON 292's RAM V12, nursed by men who had been rebuilding it since before the ration was a rumour, reached the flag more often than in any season of the horsepower era. Yepes waved the leaders through, banked the quiet finishes, and smiled the far-side smile at every sunset. The garage's low voices permitted one admission by autumn: the gap to the back of the pack was no longer a distance. It was merely a gap. The telescope stayed up.
team.moon.objective: Turn the gardening year into finishes, and the finishes into a shorter orbit.
team.moon.definingEvent: The #30 records the best finishing rate of the garage's modern memory.
team.moon.carryForward: Moon now believes the coming formula might actually reach it, and the belief is new.

team.iris.summary: The hunt was drawn up, and the rulebook refused to cooperate. The one-allocation law rewarded the patience the cathedral preached and the restraint the flame declined to practice, and Salgado spent the season fighting the gardening with fire, glorious in bursts, punished in stints. The IRIS 717 was the grid's most precise instrument, its PRISM 90 V10 the cleanest singer, but the white gloves learned that a ration cannot be out-qualified. In the shared bay, the sister house's quieter methods began looking uncomfortably like the future. The campaign was postponed, and the fire did not take it well.
team.iris.objective: Hunt the second crown on a rulebook the fire keeps refusing to read.
team.iris.definingEvent: Salgado burns a ration in a single glorious charge, and the garage holds the inquest in whispers.
team.iris.carryForward: The glasshouse bay is splitting, and the violet house intends to race the quarrel as hard as the crown.

team.azalea.summary: The frost read the allocation law as a love letter. Larssen's whole method, hold the breath, spend nothing early, be the last one cold, was now the sport's defining discipline, and the AZALEA 808 moved quietly into the season's upper midfield, the #34 banking the kind of arithmetic that makes big boards twitch. The BLOOM 88 V8's manners were impeccable. Across the shared bay, the sister house's fire kept scorching its own rubber, and the glasshouse said nothing, which said everything. The one wind tunnel still hummed for two flags, but the family could feel the wall coming.
team.azalea.objective: Bank the arithmetic the fire keeps wasting, and let the big board do the repricing itself.
team.azalea.definingEvent: Larssen out-banks the sister house across a full rationed season, and says nothing about it.
team.azalea.carryForward: The wall in the glasshouse bay is coming, and the frost will be on her own side of it.
